---
title: "Gen1: Selective Prediction (Deprecated)"
subtitle: "Early 2025 | AA Snowflake Internal Data | 14-Day Rapid Prototype"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-location: right
    toc-depth: 2
    number-sections: false
    page-layout: full
    smooth-scroll: true
---

```{=html}
<nav style="margin-bottom:1.5rem;">
  <small><a href="index.html">Home</a> › <a href="research-overview-en.html">Research Overview</a> › Gen1</small>
  <br>
  <a href="research-overview-en.html" style="display:inline-block;margin-top:0.5rem;padding:0.3rem 0.8rem;border:1px solid #ccc;border-radius:4px;text-decoration:none;font-size:0.9rem;">← Back to Research Overview</a>
</nav>
```

::: {.panel-tabset}

## Overview

**Research Goal:** "When NOT to Use Flight Delay Prediction Models"

**Core Concepts:**

- **Selective Prediction:** The model can choose to "abstain" from predicting certain samples, only making predictions for flights it is confident about
- **Slice Discovery:** Automatically identify subgroups (slices) where the model performs particularly poorly
- **Model Calibration:** Ensure predicted probabilities match actual proportions
- **Cost-based Abstention:** Decide when to abstain based on a cost function
- **Novelty Detection:** Detect anomalous samples not seen during training

**Dataset:** American Airlines Snowflake internal data, 2.7M flights

**Scale:** 30 Python scripts (code_001 to code_030)

## Key Results

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
**Selective Prediction 80% coverage:** BA = 0.9083
:::

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
**Cost-based abstention optimal point:** coverage = 18.3%, BA = 0.9390
:::

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
**Test AUC-ROC:** 0.9280
:::

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
**LSTM test BA:** 0.8638
:::

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
**FT-Transformer test BA:** 0.8642
:::

Tree-based models performed comparably to deep learning models, with significantly lower training cost.

## ETD Leakage {#etd-leakage}

::: {.callout-warning}
## FATAL ISSUE: ETD Data Leakage

**ETD (Estimated Time of Departure)** is derived from the **actual departure time**, not information available at the time of prediction.
:::

**Issue Details:**

- Correlation between ETD and arrival delay: **~0.92**
- All 9 "do-not-use" slices in Gen1 were based on ETD deviation bins (`late_5-30`, `late_30-60`, `late_>60`)
- This means the analysis is **entirely invalid** — the model was simply "seeing the answer"

**Impact:**

- AUC 0.928 is **artificially inflated** because ETD directly encodes the answer
- All selective prediction analyses and slice discovery results based on ETD are unreliable

::: {.callout-warning}
## Conclusion

Gen1 is **entirely invalidated**. Research direction shifted from "when not to use models" to "how to build models with zero leakage" → entered Gen2.
:::

:::

```{=html}
<script>
window.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {
  var hash = window.location.hash.replace('#', '');
  if (!hash) return;
  var tabs = document.querySelectorAll('[data-bs-toggle="tab"], [data-bs-toggle="pill"]');
  for (var i = 0; i < tabs.length; i++) {
    var target = tabs[i].getAttribute('data-bs-target') || tabs[i].getAttribute('href') || '';
    if (target.indexOf(hash) !== -1) {
      tabs[i].click();
      break;
    }
  }
});
</script>
```
